claims.claim: CSS Grid's ability to control rows and columns simultaneously gives it unmatched compositional freedom compared to Flexbox for bento/asymmetric layouts.
claims.quote: Grid allows controlling rows AND columns simultaneously, offering unmatched compositional freedom

claims.claim: A three-tier card size system is recommended for bento grids: feature cards (2col x 2row), wide cards (2col x 1row), and tall cards (1col x 2row).
claims.quote: Feature cards span 2 columns × 2 rows for hero content; Wide cards span 2 columns for horizontal messaging; Tall cards span 2 rows for vertical content

claims.claim: The grid-template-areas property provides a visual and intuitive syntax that makes complex bento layouts readable at a glance and easier to maintain.
claims.quote: a visual and intuitive syntax... makes layouts 'readable at a glance' and facilitates 'maintenance and modifications'

claims.claim: Responsive bento grids should reduce column count from 4 (desktop) to 2 (tablet) to 1 (mobile) with corresponding layout restructuring.
claims.quote: reducing columns from 4 (desktop) → 2 (tablet) → 1 (mobile) with corresponding layout restructuring

claims.claim: Card animations in bento grids must use transform and opacity exclusively because these are GPU-optimized and do not trigger layout recalculation.
claims.quote: Prioritize `transform` and `opacity` for your animations. These properties are GPU-optimized and do not trigger layout recalculation.
